requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must conduct qualified research activities developing or improving products, processes, software, or techniques to claim R&D credit reducing tax liability dollar-for-dollar, with special provisions for startups to offset payroll taxes
criteria: Research must meet four-part test: permitted purpose, technological uncertainty, process of experimentation, technological in nature
Qualified research expenses (QREs): wages, supplies, contract research, cloud computing costs
Activities must seek to discover information technological in nature and useful in developing new/improved business components
Includes software development, product design, process improvement, prototyping, and manufacturing optimization
Credit generally 20% of current year QREs exceeding base amount (simplified method) or 14% regular credit method
Small businesses (under $5M gross receipts, first 5 years) can apply up to $500,000 credit against payroll taxes
Can apply credit against AMT for businesses with gross receipts under $50M average of prior 3 years
Detailed documentation required: project descriptions, time tracking, financial records, technical uncertainty documentation

rd_credit_rules.credit_rate: Generally 20% of qualifying research expenses using Alternative Simplified Credit method
rd_credit_rules.payroll_tax_offset: Eligible small businesses can apply up to $500,000 credit against FICA taxes
rd_credit_rules.amt_relief: Small businesses (under $50M gross receipts) can use credit against AMT
rd_credit_rules.broad_applicability: Applies to many industries: manufacturing, software, engineering, food science, agriculture
rd_credit_rules.carryforward_carryback: Unused credits can be carried back 1 year and forward 20 years
rd_credit_rules.state_credits: Many states offer additional R&D credits doubling or tripling federal benefit

restrictions: Must reduce deduction for wages and expenses by amount of credit claimed (effective cost)
Research must be technological in nature; social sciences, humanities generally don't qualify
Funded research (customer-funded, government grants) generally not qualified unless at-risk
Must maintain contemporaneous documentation; reconstructing after audit challenging
Payroll tax offset limited to $500,000 per year and only for qualified small businesses
Complex calculations and substantiation requirements often require specialized R&D credit study
IRS audit rates higher for R&D credit claims; adequate documentation essential
After TCJA: must capitalize and amortize R&D expenses over 5 years (15 if foreign) starting 2022
